$schema: Inventory of mutating /api routes that do NOT call logChange. Each entry is either (a) a permanent volume exemption (chat/note/activity self-log) or (b) a TODO scheduled for a follow-up audit-coverage PR. Removing an entry is fine once the route either calls logChange or is deleted. Adding an entry requires a one-sentence reason that would survive code review.
app/api/projects/[id]/messages/route.ts: Chat-volume exempt — every project conversation message would drown the audit signal. Messages are immutable + visible in the project timeline anyway.
app/api/projects/[id]/messages/[messageI: Chat-volume exempt — paired with messages POST.
app/api/projects/[id]/notes/route.ts: Rep-note volume exempt — high-frequency UX, not commission-affecting.
app/api/projects/[id]/notes/[noteId]/rou: Rep-note volume exempt — paired with notes POST.
app/api/projects/[id]/activity/route.ts: Activity log IS the audit surface for project lifecycle events — auditing the audit-writer would be circular.
app/api/webhooks/resend/route.ts: Webhook receiver — no human actor to attribute. The audit signal is the EmailDelivery row itself (status / deliveredAt / bouncedAt / errorReason all written by this handler). HMAC verification gates the call.
app/api/projects/[id]/handoff/route.ts: Delegates the send to lib/handoff-service.sendInstallerHandoff, which writes logChange entries (project_handoff_send / _resend / _test_send / _auto_send). The route is auth + replay-guard only.
app/api/cron/stalled-digest/route.ts: Cron handler — no user actor. Persists nothing on success (just sends an email); structured logger.info('stalled_digest_sent') captures the run signal.
